prompt: A powerful conceptual image showing invisible inequality in air pollution. A single urban street is divided into two contrasting halves without a visible barrier. On one side, the air is clear, soft light fills the space, and people move freely. On the other side, the air is dense with heavy particulate haze, visibility is low, and people appear slowed or burdened as if breathing is difficult. The transition between the two sides is abrupt but has no physical wall, emphasizing invisible environmental inequality. No text, no charts, no symbols, no labels. Cinematic composition, strong contrast, atmospheric depth, emotional tone of unease and imbalance --no infographic elements, no maps, no globes, no numbers, no text
insight: Air pollution exposure is uneven, with some populations living in conditions that feel visibly heavier and harder to breathe than others.
